recommendation.main: Do not keep expanding generic implementation survey now; the next paper-critical gain is a deployment/browser bridge.
recommendation.first: Refresh AWS credentials and run AWS NLB + s2n-quic live forwarding echo.
recommendation.second: If AWS remains blocked, prepare a controlled public Chrome origin for media/range/upload page-ready trials.
recommendation.third: Use Safari only as PASS_FEASIBILITY after Allow remote automation is enabled.

tracks.label: AWS NLB + s2n-quic live forwarding echo
tracks.blocker: AWS identity still classifies as invalid_client_token on the current host.
tracks.needed_from_user_or_environment: Refresh AWS credentials, then run the dedicated live NLB+s2n runner before designing active path-change.
tracks.paper_value: Highest: directly answers the deployment/AWS part of the professor decision without needing iPhone.
tracks.risk: The first live run only proves forwarding echo and target consistency; s2n public active migration API remains a separate limitation.
tracks.decision: Primary next step once AWS credentials are valid.

tracks.label: Chrome desktop controlled-public media/range/upload handover
tracks.blocker: Tracked controlled-public Chrome rows are H3 baseline or negative-control evidence, not CM success; the user-provided public HTTPS origin is reachable but currently has no `Alt-Svc: h3`.
tracks.needed_from_user_or_environment: Configure the public domain or an AWS public origin with WebPKI TLS, HTTP/3, Alt-Svc, and workload endpoints, then run page-ready workload trials.
tracks.paper_value: High: bridges local browser evidence to real public-origin web workload continuity without iPhone.
tracks.risk: Even a PASS must prove single target QUIC session, path validation, path-change, and task completion together.
tracks.next_command_or_action: Prepare public origin, then run the controlled public Chrome media/range/upload wrappers.
tracks.decision: Best browser-facing next step after public origin is available.

tracks.label: nginx `quic_bpf` Linux production-routing check
tracks.blocker: Current host is macOS; Linux/root/writable `/sys/fs/bpf` gate is required.
tracks.needed_from_user_or_environment: Run on Linux or EC2 with root/capability and writable bpffs.
tracks.paper_value: Medium-high: strengthens production server routing discussion and separates loopback runtime from deployment routing.
tracks.risk: It is server-side routing evidence, not browser handover evidence.
tracks.next_command_or_action: Run `harness/scripts/run-nginx-quic-bpf-linux-demo.sh` on a suitable Linux host.
tracks.decision: Good EC2/Linux follow-up if AWS is available but s2n live is deferred.

tracks.label: OpenLiteSpeed production-like active-migration runtime
tracks.blocker: Local OpenLiteSpeed binary is missing and current macOS/disk conditions are not the right runtime environment.
tracks.needed_from_user_or_environment: Use Linux/EC2 or free/archive enough local artifact storage, then install/build OpenLiteSpeed.
tracks.paper_value: Medium: upgrades LSQUIC example app evidence toward a production-like server stack.
tracks.risk: Build/setup cost is high; result still remains server-stack evidence unless paired with browser workload.
tracks.decision: Useful, but lower priority than AWS NLB+s2n for the professor's current decision.

tracks.label: Safari desktop controlled-public baseline
tracks.blocker: Safari `Allow remote automation` is not enabled, so real WebDriver session creation fails.
tracks.needed_from_user_or_environment: Enable Safari Settings > Developer > Allow remote automation, then rerun session smoke.
tracks.paper_value: Medium: adds cross-browser feasibility, but claim ceiling remains lower than Chrome because there is no NetLog-equivalent artifact.
tracks.risk: A Safari PASS is feasibility evidence, not browser-internal single-session CM proof.
tracks.decision: Worth doing after one settings toggle, but not enough as the main paper contribution.

tracks.label: mvfst focused migration tests on Linux/Buck
tracks.blocker: Current host lacks the expected Buck/getdeps/disk setup for focused mvfst test execution.
tracks.needed_from_user_or_environment: Use a Linux builder with Buck/getdeps and sufficient disk.
tracks.paper_value: Medium-low for immediate paper direction: it strengthens implementation maturity, which is already fairly well covered.
tracks.risk: High build cost; still not browser/application continuity evidence.
tracks.next_command_or_action: Run focused BUCK targets identified by `tools/check_mvfst_migration_test_readiness.py`.
tracks.decision: Defer unless the paper needs one more large-scale implementation appendix.
